factors.summary: Management has provided forward guidance indicating high-single digit net sales growth across multiple segments, though with some margin compression in the Space segment. Overall, the outlook suggests continued growth in net sales and operating profit, particularly in Aeronautics and Missiles and Fire Control.

evidence.text: We expect our 2020 net sales to increase in the high-single digit range from 2019 levels. The projected growth is driven by increased volume at all four business areas.

We currently expect Space’s 2020 net sales to increase in the high-single digit percentage range from 2019 levels largely driven by Next Gen OPIR and Hypersonics. Operating profit in 2020 is expected to decrease in the mid-single digit percentage range as compared to 2019 driven by lower profit rate adjustments in government satellites largely driven by AEHF and lower equity earnings in 2020 compared to 2019. As a result, operating profit margin in 2020 is expected to decrease from 2019 levels.

We currently expect Aeronautics’ 2020 net sales to increase in the high-single digit percentage range from 2019 levels driven by increased volume on the F-35 program. Operating profit is also expected to increase in the high-single digit percentage range above 2019 levels. Operating profit margin for 2020 is expected to be slightly higher than 2019 levels. We continue to expect MFC’s 2020 net sales to increase in the high-single digit to low-double digit percentage range in 2020 as compared to 2019 driven by higher volume in the tactical and strike missiles and air and missile defense businesses, partially offset by a decrease in sales as a result of the divestiture of our Distributed Energy Solutions business. Operating profit is expected to increase in the mid-to-high-single digit percentage range in 2020 as compared to 2019 driven by the increase in sales volume.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: High-single digit net sales growth expected across segments, with some margin compression noted.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Active dividend payments and share repurchases, but remaining authorization raises questions about stock price confidence.

factors.summary: The company exhibits strong pricing power, as evidenced by successful price increases through risk retirements and volume growth, which have positively impacted operating profits across various programs. However, there is a note of stability in pricing power without significant mention of price changes affecting revenue or margins in other areas.

evidence.text: RMS’ net sales during the six months ended June 28, 2020 increased $255 million, or 3% compared to the same period in 2019.

Aeronautics’ operating profit during the quarter ended June 28, 2020 increased $147 million, or 25%, compared to the same period in 2019. Operating profit increased approximately $130 million for the F-35 program due to higher volume and risk retirements on production, sustainment, and development contracts. Adjustments not related to volume, including net profit booking rate adjustments, were $75 million higher in the second quarter of 2020 compared to the same period in 2019. MFC’s operating profit during the quarter end June 28, 2020 increased $43 million, or 13%, compared to the same period in 2019. Operating profit increased approximately $30 million for integrated air and missile defense programs due to increased volume on international contracts (primarily PAC-3 and THAAD); about $20 million for tactical and strike missile programs due to higher risk retirements on classified programs; and about $10 million for energy programs due to higher risk retirements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong pricing power evident from successful price increases and positive impacts on operating profits.
